140-        """Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.

144-        """
145-        content = ""
146-        for block in self.blocks:

148-                return None
149-            content += block.text
150-
151-        return content
152-
153-    @content.setter

155-        """Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.
156-
157-        Raises:

if not self.image:
            return self

        self.get_image_mimetype()

        self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)

        return self

    def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        """Return the image's mimetype, guessing and caching it if not already set.

        The guess is only attempted against the raw `image` bytes, so it won't
        trigger loading the `path` or the `url`.
        """
        if not self.image_mimetype and self.image:
            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        return self.image_mimetype

    def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:

102	def test_chat_message_legacy_roundtrip():
103	    legacy_message = {
104	        "role": MessageRole.USER,
105	        "content": "foo",
106	        "additional_kwargs": {},
107	    }
108	    m = ChatMessage(**legacy_message)
109	    assert m.model_dump() == {
110	        "additional_kwargs": {},
111	        "blocks": [{"block_type": "text", "text": "foo"}],
112	        "role": MessageRole.USER,
113	    }
114	
115	
116	def test_image_block_resolve_image(png_1px: bytes, png_1px_b64: bytes):
117	    b = ImageBlock(image=png_1px)
118	
119	    img = b.resolve_image()
120	    assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
121	    assert img.read() == png_1px
122	
123	    img = b.resolve_image(as_base64=True)
124	    assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
125	    assert img.read() == png_1px_b64
126	
127	
128	def test_image_block_resolve_image_path(
129	    tmp_path: Path, png_1px_b64: bytes, png_1px: bytes
130	):
131	    png_path = tmp_path / "test.png"
132	    png_path.write_bytes(png_1px)
133	
134	    b = ImageBlock(path=png_path)
135	    img = b.resolve_image()
136	    assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
137	    assert img.read() == png_1px
138	
139	    img = b.resolve_image(as_base64=True)
140	    assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
141	    assert img.read() == png_1px_b64
142	
143	
144	def test_image_block_resolve_image_url(png_1px_b64: bytes, png_1px: bytes):
145	    with mock.patch("llama_index.core.base.llms.types.requests") as mocked_req:
146	        url_str = "http://example.com"
147	        mocked_req.get.return_value = mock.MagicMock(content=png_1px)
148	        b = ImageBlock(url=AnyUrl(url=url_str))
149	        img = b.resolve_image()
150	        assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
151	        assert img.read() == png_1px

file.content:
def test_chat_message_legacy_roundtrip():
    legacy_message = {
        "role": MessageRole.USER,
        "content": "foo",
        "additional_kwargs": {},
    }
    m = ChatMessage(**legacy_message)
    assert m.model_dump() == {
        "additional_kwargs": {},
        "blocks": [{"block_type": "text", "text": "foo"}],
        "role": MessageRole.USER,
    }


def test_image_block_resolve_image(png_1px: bytes, png_1px_b64: bytes):
    b = ImageBlock(image=png_1px)

    img = b.resolve_image()
    assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
    assert img.read() == png_1px

    img = b.resolve_image(as_base64=True)
    assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
    assert img.read() == png_1px_b64


def test_image_block_resolve_image_path(
    tmp_path: Path, png_1px_b64: bytes, png_1px: bytes
):
    png_path = tmp_path / "test.png"
    png_path.write_bytes(png_1px)

    b = ImageBlock(path=png_path)
    img = b.resolve_image()
    assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
    assert img.read() == png_1px

    img = b.resolve_image(as_base64=True)
    assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
    assert img.read() == png_1px_b64


def test_image_block_resolve_image_url(png_1px_b64: bytes, png_1px: bytes):
    with mock.patch("llama_index.core.base.llms.types.requests") as mocked_req:
        url_str = "http://example.com"
        mocked_req.get.return_value = mock.MagicMock(content=png_1px)
        b = ImageBlock(url=AnyUrl(url=url_str))
        img = b.resolve_image()
        assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
        assert img.read() == png_1px

        img = b.resolve_image(as_base64=True)
        assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
        assert img.read() == png_1px_b64


def test_image_block_resolve_error():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="No image found in the chat message!"):
        b = ImageBlock()
        b.resolve_image()


def test_image_block_store_as_anyurl():
    url_str = "http://example.com"
    b = ImageBlock(url=url_str)
    assert b.url == AnyUrl(url=url_str)

content.text: No existing tests reference mimetype directly. Let's fix the ordering so the invariant (self.image is always base64 after construction) holds consistently in `get_image_mimetype`.

input.old_string:
if not self.image:
            return self

        self.get_image_mimetype()

        self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)

        return self

    def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        """Return the image's mimetype, guessing and caching it if not already set.

        The guess is only attempted against the raw `image` bytes, so it won't
        trigger loading the `path` or the `url`.
        """
        if not self.image_mimetype and self.image:
            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        return self.image_mimetype

            guess = filetype.guess(base64.b64decode(self.image))
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        return self.image_mimetype

51	class ImageBlock(BaseModel):

62	        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
63	        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
64	            return url
65	        return AnyUrl(url=url)
66	
67	    @model_validator(mode="after")
68	    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
69	        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.
70	
71	        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
72	        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
73	        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
74	        """

76	            return self
77

content.text:
Diff is clean and scoped to the one file. Tests pass (`llama-index-core/tests/base/llms/test_types.py`, 12/12) and manual sanity checks confirm both eager (constructor-time) and lazy (post-clear) mimetype resolution/caching work.

Summary of the change in `llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py`:
- Added `ImageBlock.get_image_mimetype()`: returns the cached mimetype if set, otherwise guesses it from the (base64-decoded) `image` bytes and caches the result on `image_mimetype`.
- `image_to_base64` (the `model_validator`) now just base64-encodes the image and delegates mimetype resolution to `get_image_mimetype()`, removing the duplicated inline guessing logic.

result:
Diff is clean and scoped to the one file. Tests pass (`llama-index-core/tests/base/llms/test_types.py`, 12/12) and manual sanity checks confirm both eager (constructor-time) and lazy (post-clear) mimetype resolution/caching work.

Summary of the change in `llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py`:
- Added `ImageBlock.get_image_mimetype()`: returns the cached mimetype if set, otherwise guesses it from the (base64-decoded) `image` bytes and caches the result on `image_mimetype`.
- `image_to_base64` (the `model_validator`) now just base64-encodes the image and delegates mimetype resolution to `get_image_mimetype()`, removing the duplicated inline guessing logic.
